title: Kiesselbach's plexus
body_html:
It is commonly known as 'Little's area'. it is located at the anteroinferior part of the nasal septum. It is a common site for epistaxis.

Kiesselbach's plexus is formed by the anastomosis of four arteries:
  • 1. Greater palatine artery (from the maxillary artery)
  • 2. Anterior ethmoidal artery (from the internal carotid artery)
  • 3. Superior labial artery (branch of the external carotid artery)
  • 4. Sphenopalatine artery (branch of the external carotid artery)
